mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
mcqs.explanation: Array access is O(1) because we can directly calculate the memory address using the formula: Base + Index × Size. No traversal needed.

mcqs.question: If an integer array starts at address 2000 and each integer takes 4 bytes, what is the address of arr[5]?
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Static arrays have fixed size and cannot be resized. Dynamic arrays (like ArrayList) can resize but require creating a new array and copying elements.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of an array?
mcqs.explanation: Inserting at the beginning requires shifting all n elements one position to the right, making it O(n).

mcqs.question: In C, what is the index of the first element in an array?
mcqs.explanation: C uses zero-based indexing, so the first element is at index 0. This is because index represents offset from the base address.

mcqs.question: Which operation has the best time complexity in an unsorted array?
mcqs.explanation: Access by index is O(1). Search is O(n), insert at middle is O(n), and delete from beginning is O(n) due to shifting.
